name: eval-coffee
description: 2-speaker casual catch-up. Turn order IS the diarization ground truth; the vocab payoff is everyday proper nouns (Tartine, Meera, Rohan, Seattle, Mission, Patagonia). Looser, faster speech than the standup.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Hey, are we still on for lunch at Tartine?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Yeah, noon works. Is Meera joining us?

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: She might. She's flying in from Seattle tonight.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Nice. Did you try that new place in the Mission?

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: The taco spot? It was incredible. Get the carnitas.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Noted. I'll bring the book you lent Rohan.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Oh, the one about Patagonia? Keep it, no rush.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Thanks. See you at noon then.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Sounds good. I'll grab us a table.
